evals.prompt: Which search intent actually converts for us? Prioritize my content by ROI, not traffic.
evals.expected_output: Should load the newest merged dataset, resolve each query's intent, attribute URL-level GA4 conversions up to the query's intent bucket, build a per-intent table (clicks, conversions, CvR, ROI per 100 clicks, share of conversions), name the best- and worst-converting intent, and recommend shifting investment toward the best intent. Saves a dated JSON report.
assertions: Triggers on intent ROI / which intent converts / prioritize by ROI language
Loads the merged dataset from data/merged (via nod-merger's load_merged)
Buckets queries into informational / commercial / transactional / navigational (plus unknown)
Attributes conversions at the URL level and rolls them up to the query's intent bucket
Reports the URL-level attribution caveat (GA4 has no query dimension; approximation)
Ranks intents by ROI proxy (conversions per 100 clicks) and names best and worst
Gives a concrete 'shift content investment toward intent X' recommendation
Saves report to data/intent-roi/{date}.json
Core analysis uses 0 NodesHub tokens

evals.prompt: Show me ROI by search intent using the demo data.
evals.expected_output: Should run analyze.py --demo against the bundled fixture, print the per-intent table, and surface transactional as the best-converting intent and informational as the worst. Runs fully offline with no key or real data.
assertions: Runs with the --demo flag against sample_intent_roi.json
Renders a per-intent table with clicks, conversions, CvR, ROI per 100 clicks, and share of conversions
Identifies transactional as the best-converting intent
Identifies informational as the worst-converting intent
Counts each landing page's conversions once (no double-counting across queries sharing a URL)
Does not call the NodesHub API

evals.prompt: My merged data has no intents yet — classify them and then tell me which intent to invest in.
evals.expected_output: Should detect that by_query rows lack an intent, use --classify to fill them via the NodesHub intent classifier (2 tokens per keyword), then aggregate and recommend. Without a key it skips classification gracefully, labels those queries 'unknown', and still produces a report.
assertions: Recognizes that intents are missing and uses --classify to fill them
Notes the 2 tokens per keyword cost of classification
Skips classification gracefully when no NodesHub key is configured and labels queries 'unknown'
Still produces and saves a report when classification is skipped
Recommends shifting investment toward the highest-ROI intent (or states none have conversions yet)
